title: Wonderful Hardware Let Down By Poor Software
text:
This review would be 5 stars if not for one very bad flaw in these headphones. The moment you make a call, the noise canceling turns off and you (and whoever you're calling) will hear everything around you – amplified – while you're on the phone. It's so bad that I have to use my old QC25 wired headphones for calls (which work perfectly for this), and only use the QC35-S2's for wireless listening to music/podcasts/tv while doing other things. This is beyond ridiculous for headphones of this price.

As for their performance purely as listening devices, the noise canceling is slightly (but only slightly) better than the wired QC25's, but the tonal range is slightly worse in the base tones. Their ability to connect to multiple devices is great, but suffers from a tendency for them to want to connect to multiple devices AT ONCE (iphone + something else, for instance), which about half the time causes no audio to be played through them at all, even though the source is muted when

title: Technical issues
text: I get a lot of stutter. It seems like I am not the only one. It actually caused issues with a FFXV for PC. For some reason when the headphones were in 2 way communications mode the game froze every couple seconds. Weird but not a big deal. The thing that kills it is the fact that the sound stutters when I am just listening to YouTube videos. I am right next to the Bluetooth receiver with updated drivers and I still get the stutter. I had the MDX1000 until they cracked and had no issues with the sound or connection. Its disappointing that Sony can figure it out but Bose cant... I guess you have to choose between poor manufacturing quality (Sony) and poor Bluetooth implementation.

title: Unpleasant glitches and early wear off
text:
I have a very unpleasant experience with this equipment. There are constant small glitches when playing from a laptop. While it works fine when playing from a smartphone, the limitation is annoying. There is a thread with more than 26 pages in Bose's support community discussing the issue.
Removing from the laptop and reinstalling worked for a while. Now it doesn't. I updated the firmware and followed all recommendations, but the problem persists.
For the very high price it charges you, Bose should provide a better support.
I do not recommend the purchase, with so many other similar options in the market.

Update:
I could make the headphones to work without glitches by disabling a couple of Bluetooth Services, namely ARfcomm, AudioWear, AudioWear Voice and Handsfree Telephony. It still presents glitches, but now it is less frequent.
However, now I have another issue. The earpads began to wear off. It is way too early for this to happen (I bought about

title: Bose is Riding Their Reputation
text:
I left a lengthy review comparing the Jabra Evolve2 85 (E285) vs Bose Quiet Comfort 35 II (QC35II) vs Bose Noise Cancelling 700 (B700) vs Sony WH-1000XM4 (SX4) (in order of when I received them) for the E285. This is a shorter version of that review, mostly focusing on QC35II and E285. This was almost entirely written up during the week I had all four headsets for direct comparisons.

Microphone and Playback
QC35II recordings were consistently louder than the E285. But the E285 did a far better job of removing background noise, as evidenced by my recordings while drumming. Muting can be done with the QC35II but it was much more difficult than just raising/lowering the boom arm of the E285.

Audio Output - DISCLAIMER - I'm not an audiophile
E285 and SX4 audio sound better than the QC35II but primarily because I can use an equalizer and increase bass (obviously this is the most important factor). Even if the QC35II had better hardware, it’s not as accessible

title: Very, very disappointed
text:
Very, very disappointed. I really wanted to love these - as I had wanted to when I purchased a pair of original QC35s. I love Bose speakers, esp. bluetooth speakers, and have Bose stereo systems in several cars I currently / recently owned (currently two Mercedes and one Audi), all of which are excellent systems.

These are the most comfortable headphones I've tried - which includes, for reference, Sennheiser HD1, Bang & Olufsen H9 (my personal favorite of all headphones), Bowers & Wilkins P7s, Bluedio, AudioMX, etc. etc. These ARE comfortable.

I do think these have the best noise-canceling in the industry, including the Sony MDR-1000X (or current WH-1000XM2). Much of that has to do with the softness and plushness of the ear pads; you'll notice that about 2/3 of the noise canceling is accomplished simply by WEARING the headphones, even while off.

I like the addition of the multifunction button that toggles between noise canceling high / low / off. Very g

title: Very good sound
text: Very good sound quality and I am pleased with the comfort, but only four stars for three reasons: no charging adapter included - seriously at this price point? The "customizable" button can only be assigned to a few things. The last reason is the charging seems slow and then the power drops down to 70 percent very soon after you start using it. It is too soon to tell if it has a defective battery.

title: Amazing headphones and really a value (believe it or not) compared to another brand.
text:
I was not sure what direction I wanted to go with travel headphones so I tried a couple of different ones. I tried these Bose QuietComfort 35 II, the QuietComfort 20 in ear, and the Sennheiser HD1. The in-ear are not for me just because of the shape and size of my ears. :( I liked them and for what they were the noise cancellation was pretty good. With the HD1 and the QC 35 the biggest difference is price. I would say the HD1 was a bit louder and I could hear some "cooler" things that I couldn't hear with any other headset, but the biggest problem with it was there was an obvious defect in the unit I received. I sent them back. But to the point of this review.

The QuietComfort 35 are scary good. The sound quality is amazing. Even the bass was great given that Bose doesn't have the biggest reputation for bass in their headphones. The noise cancellation is especially scary. It is amazing the technology that is able to allow for so much around a person to be blocked. I

title: Does not actually cancel most noise
text:
I am so disappointed. When I received this package today, I thought I was finally going to have the chance to get some sleep. I live in a room right next to a noisy person (and rude - phone calls right next to me at 2 a.m. kind of deal), and have neighbors with yappy dogs that they leave out for hours on end at times. I've tried various ear plugs, but those haven't been effective enough for blocking the noises, so I figured I'd try active noise-cancelling headphones. These do not work for that purpose. I have the noise cancelling function set on "high," but I can still hear the TV downstairs, footsteps nearby and chatting a few rooms away. It blocks has blocked out a very limited range of background road noise (noises like revving engines are still heard), but simply isn't doing what it claims to be able to do: cancel noise.

I'm desperate here - does anyone happen to have suggestions for headphones that actually cancel noise? I've been playing brown noise in the background w

title: Not an original Bose product!
text: Buttons on the side were not working and the quality of the sound was very poor. I went to the Bose store in NYC to see if my headphones were defective or if I had set up the bluetooth connection improperly. To my surprise,  I was informed by the representative that this was not an original Bose product.

title: Overpriced garbage.
text:
The sound quality and comfort of this set of headphones are pretty good even considering its relatively steep price. While it might be a bit awkward putting these on if you've never used a pair of noise-cancelling cans before (it feels a little like your ears have to pop), you quickly get used to it. The sound quality is phenomenal; I use these mostly for playing video games in addition to music, and they're simply unmatched by any other set of headphones I have, including my pair of Audio Technica studio monitors, and a pair of Sennheiser "beater" phones that I use while traveling and such. Though this is to be expected considering the price of the Bose is far more than those other pairs. Zero complaints, but I'm no audiophile, so if you're looking for an in-depth review you'll have to look elsewhere.

Being a gamer, I spend quite a few hours-long sessions with these on. My Audio Technicas had a nasty habit of really hurting my left ear, leaving soreness and a red mark if I

title: Just threw them in the trash
text: Worthless garbage.  Uncomfortable.  After 6 months, they couldn't even maintain a connection to my iPad for more than 3 minutes.  Sound quality was good when they worked.  Battery life was fine.  Quality was apparently sub-par.  Price was ridiculous.  I'm done with Bose overpriced junk.

title: These are the headphones
text: Typically my head gets sweaty with headphones, but these are too comfortable. These headphones are perfect. They really block out most sounds when you have a little song playing. It can be a little spooky at times. These headphones are perfect sound quality too, as bose does. The bass is normal, not heavy like other headphones. I enjoy that, as bass can wear out the ears over time. The app is a little minimalistic and leaves a lot to be desired. I also love how the Google Assistant on my phone functions with the button on my headphones. It is really amazing to see it work. It is also perfect with calls. I love these headphones.

title: Witchcraft Involved in Noise Cancellation?
text: I am forced to the conclusion that witchcraft must have been employed in the creation of the noise cancellation in these headphones. Just now I had nothing playing on them while lying in bed and out of curiosity I turned off the noise cancellation. I was confused when I suddenly heard a loud rumbling. I took off the headphones and realized it was my white noise machine on my bedside table. I keep it pretty loud to drown out noises from outside the house. I turned the noise cancellation back on and the sound completely disappeared. How in the world can it do that? I mean, maybe white noise is something particularly easy for them to cancel out, but that is still incredible. Is someone playing a prank on me and just turning the noise machine off as soon as I hit the noise cancellation button? I might think so, but if I lean in really close to it, a bit of the sound gets through. A seemingly unrealistically small amount... and yeah, yeah the headphones sound amazing when you play stuff in

title: Amazing comfort, crisp clear sound, and absolutely no background noise.  Superb!
text:
Bose more or less invented (with other companies working in parallel) noise cancelling headphones, and while there is an emphasis on style, marketing, and hype--these are legit. No weird sound, eerily free of background noise, and supremely comfortable. If you want the best noise cancelling headphones--these or the original QC35 are it.

The box was surprisingly small--so much so, I wondered if these weren't full sized over the ear headphones. They are, albeit with a very justified focus on folding compactly. The box opens to a minimal experience with the nearly fully charged headphones in the included case. Accessories are the case, a bizarrely shorty micro USB cable, and an analog cable. The included instructions are absolutely minimal (unless I missed the larger one I found online) and basically direct you to the app. This created a modest issue, as I was diligently charging the headphones, and lo and behold, they cannot be paired when charging. They cannot do anyt

title: Defective Firmware
text:
I am a fan of Bose headphones. They are expensive, but up until this purchase, I felt there was still a value there. The pair I got has a noisy left ear. I downloaded the firmware update. That software is junk, but I finally got it to start up. It states that I am at the current level of software.

This is a huge disappointment.

Update: I called Bose Customer Support. They asked if I had tried it on two devices. I had. That was the end of the diagnostics. They were going to send me a replacement product. There wasn't even a mention of trying a firmware update. A person on the Bose Community board had this problem over a year ago and she received a second defective product. Apparently Bose isn't the company they used to be, if they can't fix a manufacturing or QA issue in over a year.

title: They are ok
text:
Bought 1 pair and didn't care for them so I gave to my husband. Bought a 2nd pair because of crying kids and loud excited old ladies on train at christmas and didn't want to listen to them on trip home on train. My head might be a tad too small. Wish they made them in a smaller size.
They do not block out sound at all, not even close to it. It just muffleds sound. Makes sound less sharp and shrill.

title: THESE HEADPHONES SAVED MY MARRIAGE
text: Ok, I’m a HUGE Bose fan in general, but these noise canceling headphones quickly became the one thing I would never be able to live without. Don’t judge, you haven’t experienced my husband’s sleep apnea and resulting snoring (Oh. My. GOD). If I was going to a deserted island (and my husband was joining me), the one thing I couldn’t live without out while on my relaxing island getaway would be these headphones. I literally cannot sleep without them due to my husband’s snoring. I tried countless other earplugs and noise canceling buds and headphones (sometimes wearing silicone earplugs WITH headphones to no avail… I tried everything), but none of them worked; they didn’t cancel noise effectively and/or they were just stupid uncomfortable, like so uncomfortable I ended up throwing them out of my bed. I sleep in these every single night, falling asleep to the sound of raindrops. I literally cannot hear my husband come to bed or my five dogs barking when I’m wearing these. They are surprisi

title: Only works with music app, upgrade bricked it
text: These things only work with the music app. The pause feature will always revert back to that, no matter what app you were actually using - YouTube for instance. Then it took forever to download and install a firmware upgrade. After that, it won’t connect to anything. It’s a brick now. I didn’t buy them here, but at the Apple store. Hopefully I can return them. I am done with Bose.

title: These sound like crap
text: I'm a happy Bose QC 15 owner looking to replace due to a bad headphone jack connection.  Unfortunately, these sound much worse.  The high end is limited and garbled; the bass is a clear departure from the warm, powerful bass of the 15s.  These just sound weak and messy.  Very surprised.

title: A bit expensive for sound quality
text: It blocks out low frequency noise like a plane engine but I could still hear a baby crying in the background. The sound is good for music when listening via Bluetooth but the sound is only ok for movies and shows with the cable. With the rumble of the plane engine, it almost wasn't loud enough to hear the dialogue. I had to turn the volume on my tablet up all the way.

title: NO USER MANUAL IS AVAILABLE FOR THIS PRODUCT!
text: Everybody should be aware that this is a complex, sophisticated product which is shipped completely without instructions. Also, it is shipped without a charger, and nowhere mentions that the battery needs to be charged. Is it in fact rechargeable? - I am guessing so but there is no information about battery care.  The only clue is a link to the Bose website, whereupon under this product it says "There is no user manual available for this product".  By the way, I am a Ph.D in electronics of the kind who builds his own computers.  The dissatisfied, desperate purchaser of this product can download snippets of information on how to achieve various objectives. For example there is quite a useful one which tells you which control button is which.  But for $349 Bose owes the purchaser some instructions instead of leaving him/her to flail around trying to find by experiment and trial and error how to use it. I feel insulted! I wanted a product, not a research project - I have better th

title: ... that expired and now you can do a lot better. I bought 2 sets from them and their ...
text: Boise had a patent that expired and now you can do a lot better.  I bought 2 sets from them and their life was short. $600 down the toilet.  The sound is great, if not the best , their service and quality is notoriously bad.  I see from the reviews I'm not the Lone Ranger here. Grrrr.  Some companies need to die and this is one of them. Beware!

title: The incomparable Bose does it again!
text:
Bose has been and remains the best headphone maker. I’ve tried at least a dozen different products on amazon, with perhaps a dozen more in my life, and in all those experiences, the Bose name always stood far ahead of the crowd.

First, their noise cancelling technology remains the best. Eerily effective, especially on airplanes and in cars, trains, or buses, you put them on, and the world becomes quieter, making you realize how terrible the ambient noise was! These have the added benefit of adjustable noise cancellation, unlike previous models.

In comfort, these are built for long-term use, remaining wearable for hours. Most other headphones I’ve tried tend to make your ears sore, but not these! They feel soft and lovely everywhere they land on your head.

With or without Bluetooth, these offer exceptional clarity of sound, powerful bass, and clear crisp treble, none of which turn to distorted noise, even at high volumes. Yes, these are very powerful! An

title: What kind of idiot would pay $300 dollars for headphones? Me.
text:
Back when I used to spend my days fumbling with earbud wires, I wondered "What kind of idiot would pay $300 dollars for headphones, when you can hear your sounds perfectly fine for less than $10?". Even as these products rose more and more to popularity, I scoffed and resisted.

Those days are long gone. After I borrowed them from my friend for a day and tried them myself, I knew why. After getting them, these quickly became a non-negotiable part of my life. I use them to:

- take and receive meetings
- freely go pee, refill coffee, and wash my dishes while listening to a meeting
- go on runs while listening to music
- ignore noisy people around me
- get myself into a state of focus
- calm my anxiety on airplanes
- show people I am not interested to talk to them
- as a fashion statement

Seriously, I wear these so much I think I am developing a bald spot. Maybe that's why all the engineers are bald. The sound quality is s

title: Just Cancels a Few Noises - Rarely connects via bluetooth
text:
I live in a loud neighborhood and was hoping this would at least muffle some of the sounds. It doesn't. You can hear motorcycles, boom cars, sports cars, and car horns when you have them on.

These noise 'cancelling' headphones only cancel out some noises, like general ambient noise. I guess it's just used for people on airplanes.

And the bluetooth connectivity is awful with Apple products. Be ready to constantly remove/add/update bluetooth devices with this hot mess of bluetooth technology.

title: Disposable!!!
text:
Bose used to make their noise cancelling headphones so that they could accommodate a standard AAA battery. The QC 2, QC 15, and QC 25 all used a single AAA battery and would last for 15+ hours. When I went on a flight, I'd carry two Eneloop rechargeable batteries with me, and if one died, I could just remove the dead one and put a fresh one in.

Now, they use an internal, non-replaceable lithium ion battery. So, when the battery reaches the end of its usable lifespan, you must throw the entire product away. What a waste! Even worse, if it happens while you're on a flight, that's just too bad. No noise cancelling for you!

Oh, and let's not forget that lithium ion batteries occasionally catch fire, and sometimes even explode. It's definitely not a good idea to have one next to your head, or on an airplane for that matter.

title: Overall a reliably good headphone
text:
Sound quality is well rounded - though I usually prefer something a bit bass-heavy, this hasn't been unusable by any means.
They are pretty comfortable to wear all day - not totally pain free, and a reminder that wearing glasses with any headphones is usually uncomfortable at the end of the day because of the cuffs pressing your frames into your head.
The battery life has been totally sufficient between transitioning from student to full time employee on the 9-5 grind. I charge every other day or so.
The noise cancellation is great for sitting in a crowded noisy place like a study area or airplane or coffee shop.

They don't look that bad - I thought I would hate the bulkiness more, but it's not very bad. I have a pretty small head and am pretty petite overall.

title: Overrated, especially by youtubers.
text:
I didn't get it from Amazon. But still want to publish this review here.
This headphone is overrated partially because of the social media hype.
Is it good? Yes. The ANC feature is awesome. It is comfortable in cool weather. The sound is better than average headphones. It supports multi device connections (2 for normal usages, 3 if you count the google assistant) which is super convenient comparing to 1000XM2.
Is it great? Not at all.
First of all, when people talk about this headphone people say how comfortable it is. But it is not that much more comfortable than other headphones such as 1000XM2. Prepare to have a lot of sweat around your ears in summer even in a room with AC.
Second, the sound quality is good only when compared to the "average" headphones/earbuds such as the one that comes with iPhone. It sounds like a disaster even when compared with a JBL Everest 300: flat resolution, messed-up bass. and tasteless overall performance.
Third, th

title: I am in LOVE. These have changed my life and my marriage
text: I'm a writer and my husband plays his tv shows and sports or music which really detracts me. I must have ADD or something because I get distracted so easily. In the room I have this ceiling fan that does not know how to move air quietly, the "special silent" motor isn't silent anyore—unless I put on these headphones! I have no idea how they work, but bluetooth connects them to my computer and my phone so I can use them like regular bluetooth speaker headphones. It's summer now and my neighbors always have bbqs with lots of beer. I like to sit by my pool with a glass of wine and write. I can't even hear any of them unless someone shouts I got the silvery ones and am glad I did. They sort of disappear on my head easier than big ol black ones. I have never had pain from these either and wear them all day. When I bought them, I also bought the Sony Noise Cancelling Headphones WH1000XM3 which are actually just as amazing but Bose was just one tiny bit better. But mostly, I didn't like the d

title: Be Careful bad customer service
text: They broke in 4 months, called Bose and they wouldn't replace them. I would stay away.

title: Great Product, Horrible Support
text:
Quality of sound and comfort is great- but the warranty is not. I first broke these headphones by stepping on them and snapping the band. Bose said they'd discount me for a new pair- but I still had to shell out another $200 for a new pair. Two years after buying that new pair, and now suddenly the right ear is not working any more. I didn't do anything wrong, the audio just got quieter and quieter, and now it hardly works at all.

The customer service support system is awful. They keep directing me to the "discounted" price, and not actually doing anything to provide support because of a one-year warranty. I'm sorry, but your $300 headphones should last longer than two years without breaking on their own accord. I am absolutely disappointed because I really love these headphones, but I don't want to shell out another small fortune just for them to break like they're made of glass.

title: Great hardware & sound; pitiful software and app makes all wireless features useless
text:
UPDATE February 2019: The latest software update eliminated most of the problems with hijacking my hearing aids and linking with my iPhone is now more reliable. Unfortunately, it is now virtually impossible to connect to my MacBookPro unless I do a hard reboot of both laptop and headphones. That makes the Bose phones useless for videoconferencing, listening to music via laptop, or pretty much anything headphones are routinely used for. It appears that the software engineering at Bose continues to be second rate, and if problems aren't fixed a year after the product was sold - I fear that they never will. This is the last Bose product I will consider...

UPDATE December 2018: None of the problems from my original review have been addressed by Bose despite multiple app updates. The implementation of Bluetooth connection on these phones is poor and unreliable; the associated app features are terrible and non-functional. Lowered review from three to two stars now that it'

title: Great on Sound, Bad on Size
text:
These headphones are great on sound and noise reduction (average user, main use is in a open office space)-- HOWEVER I had to return them.

I have a small head (like wear a child's size large/x-large in helmets), size 7 in hats, and these headphones on the smallest setting "hang" on the top of my head to stay on, making it uncomfortable. They don't clamp in enough because they are too big, and slip out of place any time I bend over or look up. I'm so sad since I love Bose and have Bose ear buds since they are some of the most secure ear buds out there for small little ears like mine.

They fail in fitting petite heads, wish they were adjustable down to a smaller size of just 1/2-1 inch more and they would be perfect. So sad these didn't work out.

title: Do not buy
text:
I read all reviews, I said, well maybe they are exaggerating...I bought the headphones...
The sound is substandard in comparison to other less sophisticated products from Bose. I like the brand, but this product is not good on sound quality, not even comparing to cheap headphones.
Noise cancelling is good.... meh.

title: Was working fine until the update
text: The noise cancellation feature has been destroyed by the last update. I am really really disappointed. It's been a month with no official resolution from Bose. I strongly recommend you to stay away from Bose until they get their <thing> together and fix this. I am not into conspiracy theories and I am truly hopeful that this screw-up was not on purpose in order to sell the newer headphones.

title: Great for large heads and big ears!
text: Love these headphones! I have a large head and ears and they are super comfortable even after 4 or 5 hours of continuous wear. The noise canceling is amazing, I sit about 6 feet from a window AC and don't hear even a whisper from it with these on!

title: Mobile Devices ONLY!
text: I was really disappointed that these headphones could not be used on an all in one HP computer. I researched this seemingly forever, and I was never ever to resolve the issue. They simply would not connect to the device. Even for use on a laptop, it is very difficult to setup, and some laptops never connect to the headphones. I was however, able to easily connect the headphones to my Apple Ipad. These headphones are far too limited in terms of their bluetooth connection ability. But once connected, they are wonderful to use.

title: Soooo great
text:
Omg this has changed my life
I have hearing damage and this makes me feel better

title: Love having these for travel
text: Pricey but still a good investment. Airline travel is so stressful, but when you can block out all the airplane noise with these headphones, it makes it  much easier. Didn’t give it 5 stars only because with my glasses on, it can get a little uncomfortable after a while.

title: It’s Bose or nothing.
text:
I purchased mine refurbished from Bose after purchasing and returning 3 different sub $100 headsets here on Amazon. These check all the boxes, great sounding, comfortable, lightweight and noise canceling that really works. I tried to save money but everything else had one or two major drawbacks or flaws.
The only other product that sounds as good were my AirPod Pros. I’d say the AirPods are slightly better at noise cancellation but I couldn't get my right one to seal in my ear canal so they’re history.

title: Fantastic Headset
text:
I work in a data center and my amazing boss decided to use part of his budget to buy these for the team after noise concerns arose a few months ago. Hence why my purchase isn't verified. They've been so good that I wanted to come leave a review.

I had to get used to the noise cancellation at first. It was such a shocking difference between my Sony MDR-7506 set. These are the first $300+ pair of headphones I've ever used and "impressed" doesn't begin to cover it. The lab I work in gets pretty loud, to talk to others you have to raise your voice no matter the distance. More than 10 ft away and the best you can do is shout "HEY!". Not only do these cancel out a good deal of it, the sound quality is also superb.

I was also surprised to find that I could make phone calls in this noisy environment and without complaint from my conversation partner. This headset really is a step up.

The battery life is superb as well, it charges quickly and I can have my heads

title: Pretty much had it with the cheaply made Bose
text:
If you think this headphone is superior to the Sony WH-1000xm2, you must be deaf, dumb, and blind. Tried both back-to-back and the Bose seems like a flimsy toy, with half a dozen screws visible. The Sony is beefier, with plusher ear cushions, and just more classy looking as no bolts or screws are visible. The headphone cable is twice as thick as the thin, cheap-looking cable that comes with the Bose. Even the marketing of the Sony product is superior, with a thicker cardboard box and nicer advertising graphics.
The Sony isn't as comfortable because it fits tighter than the Bose, with massive earcup cushions compared to the Bose. The Sony headphone is larger and feels more solid but can fold up about as compact as the Bose due to one headphone being able to swivel. I found the Bluetooth was quicker and more consistent in hooking up with my laptop. The Sony is quite irresistible due to it's high-tech features and quality build.
In regards to the noise cancellation, they are clo

title: Cancel unwanted noise!  Amen
text: The noises cancellation works wonders when the old lady is nagging! Truly comfortable headset with great sound and Alexa voice control  its worth every dollar 💵

title: Great headset
text: I'm very happy with this purchase.  Great audio quality, noise cancellation, and battery life.  I do wish it had a USB-C charge port instead of micro-USB, but that's my only complaint.

title: Great
text: Vary nice. Only down fall is can’t walk to far from were your connected.

title: Saved a Father Daughter Relationship
text: I got these for my father, who is hard of hearing, and loves to listen to LOUD podcasts on the front porch :).  He claimed to "hate headphones" and claimed he "couldn't stand wearing those dammed things."  I researched best headphones for people with sensitive ears and hearing loss.  These came up and had excellent reviews.  I left them laying around the house for a few days and he took the bait.  He can't resist trying a new gadget (he's 80).  He tried them a couple of times and promptly ordered his own pair (which ironically had gone down in price $60 since I bought them the week previously).  It's a dream come true for all who live with him.  We no longer have to hear podcasts blaring all over the house, and he claims he can no longer hear the traffic go by or the airplanes over head.  We are a happy household now.  They were worth EVERY penny (and yes...I kept my much more expensive, identical set).

title: Lost one noise canceling level after firmware update.
text:
After updating the firmware, there is no longer a high noise canceling level. When I first opened and used this headset, it was heaven; there were low, medium, and high noise canceling levels. They did and still sounds great, but I spent a lot of money for the full N/C feature.

Tip: do not update the firmware (that means don't pair the headset to the Bose app) no matter what Bose says. Don't even download the app.

title: Wonderful noise cancelation
text: Bought these for 100 dollars used off another website, but I would definitely say they're worth full price. The ability to turn ANC off is a plus, they're very neutral in sound, but there is decent bass. Only bummer is that you cannot customize the EQ with the app.

title: I have a question. With the option of “used”
text: I have a question. With the option of “used” does it mean the headphones are freaking bent bad and broken out of shape to the point of this is what I paid still 200 dollars for? I want the used “condition like new” like it said in the description.

title: Do quite a bit of traveling
text: I can say this does an amazing job at blocking out engine noises and people.

title: not great for voice, buggy firmware updates
text: I seriously use to love BOSE. The buggy firmware updates have forced me to write a review to warn AGAINST this product. There's also now weird echo sounds when you use them as a headset. Which makes them almost unusable as anything but a headset. I don't know what happened to this company and it's sad when there's so much more competition now in the market at both the low price and high-end noise cancellation space. Please please please BOSE fix the firmware and return these to a good product.

title: He has to wear all day for work and he is very pleased..
text: my husband loves these... He has to wear all day for work and he is very pleased...

title: I have not been able to get them set up.
text: There were no directions for set up, and what I did find did not work. I am going to the Bose store to see if they can help. There was no one to call for help. If I can not get them to function, I will return.

title: Never knew what I was missing
text:
These are phenomenal headphones. I didn't realize the kinds of ambient noise I had around me until after I used this for a while. I bought other fancy headphones in the past, which usually had cables and ended up broken when I tripped across it. These are wireless, with a volume control built in. (You can still plug them in if you like, but I never do that.)

When I got them in the mail, they worked perfectly. Then, after an update, for a while, the software was a bit glitchy and would randomly beep. But at some point they must've fixed that in another update, after a year or so. Because they work perfectly again.

One downside is that the microphone isn't really worth having on it. In order to use the microphone, you have to put the headphones into "headset" "communications device" mode, which doesn't properly do sound cancelling. Worse, it sounds like someone is playing back the audio from outside the headphones. I never use that mode, because I can't stand it.

title: Infinitely better software than Sony
text:
Bought and returned a Sony 1000XM3 after discovering how terrible the mic is, and how incredibly frustrating the bluetooth connections are with every device I've tried.

Got one of these instead. Immediately happier with it in every way, except that I think Sony's noise canceling is slightly better (though this is still excellent). It installs updates in less than an hour (seriously!), connects to two devices reliably, can switch devices without forcing you to disconnect manually or sometimes forget a connection to stop re-connection, and you can do a fair bit from the app that's handy (e.g. change the second device it's connected to, quickly and easily).

Sound quality is roughly on par with the 1000XM3, but mic and everything to do with bluetooth are incomparably better.

title: They sound good and the noise cancelling is great
text:
These are so expensive but what can a person do? It is hard to pass up the benefits. The comfort is great although ears do feel warm after a time. The ambient noise is greatly reduced. The sound is OK for me, but I am no audiophile. They are big and a little clunky and I can't wear them for more than an hour without airing out my ears for a few minutes. They seal quite well around the ears and there is no air flow, I suppose to make the sound cancelling work. Oh well, tradeoffs.
I find the battery lasts all day at the very least. I don't do any telephone conversations with these as a rule but a time or two and I did receive a call while using them and it went OK. I don't use the Google or the Alexa silliness but I suppose younger folks might like that. I do like that I can control the volume very easily with the control. Also, I have no problem with connecting via Bluetooth with my iPad and my android phone. It just works. I wish I could pair more than two devices at a time, but

title: Fantastic !!
text: Because of price, I purchased a headset for $79 that had ANC and Bluetooth. I returned it as the ANC didn't work and the Bluetooth kept cutting out. I then decided to go to the other extreme and got the Bose headset. The difference was like night and day. ANC is almost perfect, sound quality is excellent. Bluetooth has not cut out and phone calls are crystal clear. Alexa works very well.  I highly recommend this headset.

title: Works wonderful with the Crystal Mic setup in a B747
text: Love my QC35's as a work headset with the Crystal Mic and then as a noise cancelling headset when just traveling as a passenger.

title: Great even for big ears
text:
I originally got the Sony xm3 but I had to return them because I have big ears and my ears would be rubbing against them so I returned them for these and these are extremely comfortable and even though the Sony’s are a little better noise canceling and have a lot of extra features I really like these
And a feature that would make me get these over the Sony’s any day is the Sony’s you can only connect either your phone or computer not both but these you can connect both the your phone and computer and just pause on one and play from the other

title: Amazing! Heavy duty
text: Amazing! Heavy duty. I’m not worried about rough housing these and I use them for my daily commute. Travel case is good and easy to open and durable. I have a different pair of headphones for work and the case is cumbersome. Not these! Great noise cancellation. I like the option to have high, low, or none. Walking on the streets is good to hear some traffics noise. These really block out noise so well and sound quality is awesome. I haven’t had to charge them yet and it’s been almost 2 weeks.

title: Incredible Technology
text: I have owned several cheaper noise cancellation headphones. The all had this background buzzing noise that was not tolerable. These headphones have the most incredible noise cancellation I have ever heard. Very soft earmuffs for long flights and great sound quality you would expect from Bose. Worth the investment for traveling.

title: I cannot imagine flying without it
text: Great for travel or office use--anytime you need a moment of quiet. I like the sound more than I liked the sound quality on a comparably priced competitor.

title: Like a private live concert...
text: As soon as I turned these on, I knew I'd made the right call. 👍💥

title: Excellent headphones; outrageous price
text:
This is my fourth pair of Bose NC headphones, and my first with Bluetooth built in. They are the best so far. I have some QC15s that still do excellent Noise cancellation, but crackle when you try and listen to music.

For what Bose charges for these, one might hope that they would be made with long-lasting materials, and would be repairable. “No”, on both counts.

title: Good headphone for Price
text: Headphones have good quality of sound but fit a little loose over my ears.

title: Love love love
text: Love them. Use them on noise-cancelling at my damn loud gym to keep my sanity and hearing. Would caution against using them outside though. Couldn’t hear the world outside my own head.

title: Love it
text: Excellent for all things except functioning as a mic.

title: ... a pair of these on an impulse and I love them. They're wireless
text:
I bought a pair of these on an impulse and I love them. They're wireless, they have a great battery life, they're comfortable and the sound quality is great. They block out all sound when music is playing though, so if you're jumpy, it may be best to turn down the volume or wear it with one ear partially out of the cup. I wore it on a trip to China and watched movies comfortably the entire flight.

I also use them at work, which is actually slightly problematic because I can't tell when someone has walked into my office and has been talking to me for several seconds if I'm working with my head down.

title: Love mine
text: I love these headphones. I am 65 and I so enjoy listening to books on mine. They fit well and are very comfortable. I use them at the gym when I am on the treadmill. Makes time go by much faster! Easy to use and I'm so glad I got them!

title: Great Headphones
text:
They are great sounding, noise cancelling headphones with awesome battery life!
I use them while mowing with a zero turn "noisy" mower and only hear the music!
Love them!!!

title: My 2nd pair!
text: I still have the Bose QC I bought 7 or 8 years ago. It still works great but the ear pads starting to look raggedy, so I bought a new pair. Before buying the 2nd one, I tried the cheaper Studio3 by Beats, it doesn't stand up to the Bose, it is inferior and you can hear it.

title: Love them, i want a second pair :)
text: omg these are awesome, we love them.

title: Overall disappointment
text:
Was very excited for those, thought I got a deal when I got them for "only" 200$ on prime day. So
Sound quality is meh even without noise cancellation.
Bluetooth connectivity has constant issues
Noise cancellation is ok
Dealbreaker: They will not work when charging. That is absolutely ******** It is the first time I hear of ANY electronic device tuat will not work while charging

title: Life Saver for Me
text:
I have reviewed my review after having these a good while now and have come to the conclusion, though I really could not afford the price tag, it has been well worth it for me here in this place.
If I had to listen to the noise at full volume here, I surely would have gone mad by now, so...

They do have the good sound cancellation however not perfect, but have been a life saver in some respects in my situation here.
Not sure what causes it, but they do cut out now and again.
The one thing I will say is that they are comfortable and do fit around the ear.

If anyone needs an extra level of sound cancellation, here is a tip of what I do.
Sometimes when the noise level is at the insane level here, I am listening to an audio book on YouTube..good, then I also am listening to one of those brown noise videos..say for instance a 10 hr video on top of the vocal video. So running two videos at the same time, totally soothes me and blocks out the noise wi

title: Not the greatest quality
text: We expected the very top quality for the price we paid. The headphones fell apart after a year of use. Very disappointed. I would not recommend.

title: Great Headphones!
text: They're Bose so you know they will be good! Sound quality is great and NC is very good. (I wear glasses and may not be getting a complete seal.) Connection to any device is easy with the app. The AR feature is fantastic and I hope Bose expands on this feature. The battery life is very good. When I received mine in July, they were 80% charged and I've only had to charge them twice and use them almost daily. Would recommend.

title: Not great for sensitive hearing
text: I get migraines and because of those, I am easily annoyed by noise. I was thrilled to get these as a present. They're comfortable (not too squeezy for my head that hates things that are too tight). As for the noise canceling, I'm underwhelmed. With these on and my music playing, I can still hear a fair amount of what's going on around me, even when I'm at home and not out someplace loud. For the price, I expected these to be a lot better at noise cancelling.

title: Wear outside at your own risk!
text: The headphones have pretty decent sound my only beef is that they sit loosely on the head. The fit isn't snug enough to my liking. I had them on while outside and made a sudden movement and fell off my head. Needless to say scratches were everywhere on my new headphones....

title: Love it
text: Bought on sale for wife as a present. Performs well over last two years. Quality product as usual from Bose.

title: Works as advertised
text:
Works as advertised. I do have a big head an the ear pads get a little uncomfortable after a few hours, but has been true for every headphones I have had. My Shure headphones sound better but are plastic and I have broken twice. These seem to be made much better.

Noise cancellation is awesome on a plane or in my house when I am just trying read and my kids are running around.

title: Good value & won't be disappointed
text:
I did a lot of research on a solid pair of noise-cancelling headphones and it came down to these and another pair. I knew that these were significantly more expensive than the other, but based on a few reviews indicating that the quality of the other could be a hit or miss, I just ended up going with these because I knew they would be a solid purchase and love them. I also went to Best Buy and tried on several in the same category, and loved the lower profile of these and the lighter band around the head (didn't feel too heavy).

It was a plus to have the Google Assistant function with the series ii since it went along with the rest of my environment. I love the cordless and bluetooth feature, and I can be connected to my phone and computer at the same time. I also kind of like the robotic voice that tells me how much it's charged when I turn them on (though I think you can turn that off as well). It has high, low, and off levels of noise cancelling and also comes with a smoo

title: The Best at Noise Cancelling
text: These pair of headphone are only the best at cancelling outside noise (personally tested vs other competitors like Sony) but also the most comfortable at wearing due to deeper around-the-ear earcups and light weight. The sound is very clear and perfect for listening to vocal and instrumental music. Not for bassheads that's for sure.

title: Personally the most comfortable NC headphones on the market.
text:
So previously I bought Sony’s new noise canceling headphones that most people compare with Bose's Q35 2. Both of them were around the same price on Cyber Monday which I was able to have both of them and compare whether which one was actually better. After using both of them for a while, the winner between the two came out to be Bose all the way. The strongest point Sony couldn't follow was the comfort feeling only Bose could offer that other company products just could not even attempt to follow.
However, it is also honorable to mention that Sony has some more stronger parts in terms of noice canceling and app features you can utilize. In terms of the bass also it is quite noticeable that Sony has a stronger and wider bass compared to a more middle focused and balanced sound Bose offers to the users.
Back to the idea of the comfort feeling, Sony's headphone failed to do so mostly because of the short distance it has with the cushion and the ear. This in the end generates heat

title: Sound quality is great and the sound cancelling is magical
text:
I'm in the Air Force. I initially bought these just for long flights on TDY or deployment, but I use them all the time just walking around. Sound quality is great and the sound cancelling is magical.

The only issue is sometimes one ear will rumble if not fully charged. Also it can't really handle more than two devices at a time. Which I consider to be acceptable trade offs.

title: Bose headphones do what they say they will do
text: This is an upgrade from my first Bose noise cancelling headphones which I have used for 10 years and now my daughter is using, so I felt comfortable buying a blue tooth set; so nice without cords and the sound is amazing! The battery charge has been great, better than I expected.

title: They're good, but they're not what they COULD be.
text:
Figuring out what you use this headphone for is super important as far as whether or not you will like it.

Buy
Other
Sound
Equiptment

Presents: Quiet Comfort 35 (The second generation)

Let's talk about the good.
Noise Cancellation - To this day, to my knowledge it's still the best. I haven't heard of or listened to anything that's better. The Sony MDR-1000x is probably it's closest competitor (if not the Sennheiser NC model) and it's close but these are IMO, objectively better.

Battery Life - I don't know what wizardry they manage to pack in there for the battery but it really is amazing. WITH THAT SAID, leaving it on will drain it regardless, so make sure to flip that switch and shut them off or how awesome the battery is, isn't going to matter.

The Sound (with noise cancelling on) - I don't know why it's so blatantly different. But with the NC on, they actually sound good enough to enjoy most types of mus

title: Good headphones!
text: I sleep with these on listening to sleep sounds for insomnia on YouTube it plays all night. Drowns out the snoring going on next to me. They connect with Bluetooth. One charge lasts me  two full nights.

title: Worth the price!
text: They are lighter than beats. They fit better over my large ears. They connect and work fine with Iphone XS. They charge quickly. Only complaint is that the fake leather cups seal off air flow so my ears tend to get a little hot but thats the same with beats and others. Would be nice if foam cups were an option. One star off only for that. Has buttons for controlling audio and for controlling levels of noise cancelation. Recommend these!

title: It is loose on my head unless I put it tightly, then it messes up my hair. The charging cable stopped working after a few times.
text: Good noise cancelling, so-so sound, and poor design

title: I will NEVER buy Bose again.
text: This product must be meant for indoor typing folks who just can't stand the hum of a fan or a fly that keeps swarming because that's about all they are good for. Yes they cancel some light noises, but very very little. They are not for anyone who really wants to get hearing protection. Also, if you work outside, DO NOT BUY THESE!!!!! You can hear the wind in your ear magnified by the "noise cancellation" of the headphones. I was so sad because I hoped they would help me retain my hearing as a farmer, but they are the pits. I put some $10 ear buds in and put some $10 headphones over and I had 6 times better "noise cancellation" and no wind noise. They are comfy and shinny, so that's a nice perk for you people who care about those things, but other than that you'd be better off with some Sony earbuds from Meijer.

title: The BEST value! Couldn’t live without them. (6 month review)
text:
I’ve been using these for 6 months and they are invaluable to me. They are quiet enough for doing loud chores like mowing the lawn or vacuuming.
I also use them while I commute on the train. Even with the noise of the train cars and other people around me I’m still able to make phone calls while listening and speaking clearly with these headphones.
I love music and they are also loud enough for my taste (I’m moderately picky about audio).
Battery life is great it’ll last me all day listening to podcasts, music, and making calls.
They seem to charge back to %100 very quickly
The buttons are easy to use. They make answering calls or pausing/skipping songs easy.
They also come with a very nice case that I use often.
Overall these headphones are great and well worth the money in my option.
I would absolutely recommend these to a friend.

title: Bought 2nd Bose QC 35 Headphones, excellent sound
text: This is a 2nd Headphones. Now my wife and I both have the headphones. The sound is good, wears comfortably on the head. Battery Life is good.Pairs with other Bluetooth devices easily.

title: Ok but a little over rated
text:
I feel like they're a little over rated, yes they cancel out noise better than most of the competition but the sound hasn't exactly blown me away.

Also, im always Taking public transportation to get to work, the bus goes through the freeway and as soon as the road gets bumpy I start to hear stutters of air which ruins the sound and cuts it off.

title: Loved them Immediately
text:
Very sleek design and the comfort is amazing.

Noise canceling is so great that I wouldn’t use option while walking.

title: Nice headset but broke after 7 months
text: Worked great for about 6 months. Then one dat I was in a meeting and everyone told me they can hear everything around me. Just stopped working. I would not purchase again.

title: The sound reproduction is EXCELLENT.  They always connect, very comfortable on the ears, not hot or heavy.
text: WOW... I use them to listen as I watch movies on my Microsoft Surface and the sound reproduction is EXCELLENT. Plus they always connect, very comfortable on the ears, not hot, not heavy and wow, they really eliminate the outside ambient noise. I'm pretty impressed by how capable or effective the noise cancelling feature is. I choose the silver and no one plays with them. My guess is they don't look impressive or high tech enough. Works for me....

title: Not good for Noise cancellation.
text: This is not a good headphone for Noise cancellation. I can hear other people talk. I was expecting silence. But I like the sound quality.

title: My hands down favorites, and I've tried a lot
text:
I have a war torn battlefield of bluetooth headsets on my desk and scattered around. I have $20 ones, I have $200 ones. I have ones I like, ones I hate, most in the middle.

This is the first set I loved.

The noise cancellation is great. The sound is excellent. They're comfortable for all day wear (6-8+ hours). They connect seamlessly to everything (you'd think this was a given...trust me, it is not). The range is fabulous. They connect to multiple devices easily, meaning I can be wearing them listening to music on my computer, keep them on as I walk out of the house and start up a podcast, and not touch them once. I just love love love these headphones. I wish I could go back in time and tell myself not to bother with any others. These are the ones.

title: Comfortable but very flawed
text:
The good - very comfortable for long periods of listening, battery lasts a good amount of time (I use these for lots of plane travel), great noise cancellation

The bad - the bluetooth pairing is so frustrating. it randomly picks devices sometimes if you have paired to more than 2 devices. it only connects with 2 devices at a time but even then it doesn't always work - if you pair with your PC and iPhone, it will never play music from your iPhone. it's so frustrating because you can only control what it pairs to using the app, which is useless when I want to listen to other devices unless I'm also paired with my phone. Additionally, if I deselect a device on the app, it will still connect to it when I turn the headphones back on.

TLDR
Get these if you only want to pair with a single device. Otherwise, the pairing controls and app are a complete, unstable nightmare.

title: High quality product
text: Absolutely love them. I’ve had them for a week so far. The battery life is about a full day with the noise canceling on high. I enjoy the sound quality and the noise canceling is fantastic; i can still hear a voice if they are right next to me, but in general it blocks out a significant portion of the office noise around me. The build is sturdy and the case is just the right size
